name: Mutual Exclusion
generated_spec:
---- MODULE MutualExclusion ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N   (* number of processes *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables -----------------------------------------------------------

VARIABLES procState    (* mapping from each process id to its current state *), procState

vars == <<procState>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* State definitions -----------------------------------------------

Idle      == "idle"
Trying    == "trying"
Critical  == "critical"

StatesSet == { Idle , Trying , Critical }

ProcessIds == DOMAIN [i |-> i IN 1 .. N]

TypeOk ==
     /\ Domain(procState) = ProcessIds
     /\  \A  p : p \in ProcessIds => 
          procState[p] \in StatesSet

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Initial condition ----------------------------------------------

Init ==
     TypeOk /\
     (\E c \in ProcessIds :
        (/\ procState[c] #= Critical
         /\ UNCHANGED <<(UNUSED)>>))

(* The initial assignment ensures that no process starts already in the critical section.  
   All other states are unconstrained but must be one of the allowed values.
*)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Next-state relation ---------------------------------------------

Next ==
     \/ TRYING_TO_CRITICAL
     \/ CRIT_EXITED
     \/ Terminating

TRYING_TO_CRITICAL ==
       LET P == CHOOSE p WHERE procState[p] = Idle
           Qs == {q: q \in ProcessIds & q /= P}
       IN
            /\ procState'[P] = Trying
            /\ UnchangedExcept(P)


                            ((o <> P) /\ (procState[o] = Critical))
       IN
            /\ !OthersInCrit  (* ensure at most one can enter *)
            /\ procState'[P] = Critical
            /\ UnchangedExcept(P)

CRIT_EXITED ==
      LET C == CHOICE p : procState[p] = Critical 
          InTryOther != FALSE
              ==  \E  t \in ProcessIds :
                    ((t #= C)
                     /\\ procState[t] = Trying )
      IN
        /\ UNCHANGED <<(UNUSED)>>
        /\ IF InTryOther THEN
             /* If another process is trying, it may proceed to critical */
             TRUE   (* placeholder for potential transition actual logic omitted *)

             /* No other processes are in the "trying" state – release CS */
             procState'[C] = Idle

Terminating ==
     /\ UNCHANGED vars

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

====

name: Two-Phase Commit
generated_spec:
---- MODULE TwoPhaseCommit ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT Participants
\* Assume each identifier is from {1 .. P} for some constant P
CONSTDEF 
    ParticipantSet == [p \in Participants |-> p]

VARIABLES 
    phase          ,\\ Current phase of the transaction ("init","prepare","commit","abort"), messages, msgs, votes
    votes         ,\\ Mapping from participants to their last received message type or NONE
    messages      ,\\ Multiset of pending messages in transit elements are tuples <sender, receiver, msgType>

(* Message types *)
MessageTypes == {"Prepare", "VoteYes", "VoteNo", "Commit", "Abort"}

(* Helper constants *)
NONE  == <<>> 

(********************************************************************)
*** Type invariant (ensures variables stay within finite domains) ***
(********************************************************************)

TypeOK ==
    /\ phase \in {"init", "prepare", "commit", "abort"}
    /\ votes \in SUBSET[Participants -> MessageTypes]
       (* Each entry maps a participant to one of the defined message types *)
    /\ UNCHANGED @votes[*] \/ (\A v : Votes[v]) #v = NONE => FALSE
    /\ messages \subseteq [{ sender: ANY,
                             receiver: ANY,
                             kind: MessageTypes }]

(********************************************************************
 *** Initial state specification                                 *****
 *******************************************************************)  

Init ==
    /\ phase   = "init"
    /\ votes   = {}
    /\ messages =
        {<"Coordinator", p, "Prepare"> | p \in Participants}

(*********************************************************************
 ***** Next-state relation definition                               *
 * All disjuncts must specify ALL vars either by prime assignment 
 * or using UNCHANGED.                                            *
 *********************************************************************)

Next ==
    LET
      sendMsg(s,r,m) == < s , r , m >

      deliver(msg) ==
          /\ msg.kind \in MessageTypes
          /\ IF msg.receiver = "Coordinator" THEN
                CASE msg.sender \in Participants ->
                    /\ msgs'  = [msgKind IN MessageTypes |
                                  (IF msgKind = "VoteYes" OR msgKind="VoteNo") =>
                                      IF EXISTS x \in participants :
                                         ((x,msgKind)=msg)
                                     THEN [msgs EXCEPT ![msgKind]=TRUE]

                   (* Coordinator updates its vote record *)
              /\\ UNCHANGED <<phase,votes>>
            [] TRUE -> UNCHANGED <<phase,votes>>  

          \/ IF msg.receiver \in Participants THEN
                 /\ IF msg.kind ="Commit" THEN
                        /* Participant commits */
                     /\ UNCHANGED <<votes,messages>>
                  /\ phase'=phase
               /\ votes'=votes
             /\ messages'=UNION(messages,\{sendMsg(msg.receiver,"Coordinator","Ack"\})\}


        in
     /\ (\E p : p \in Participants & NOT p \in DOMAIN(votes)) 
         /\ messageToSend? := {<p,"Coordinator",MessageTypes> | ...}   \\ placeholder for sending Vote requests?

(*********************************************************************
 ***** Termination condition to avoid deadlock                      *
 *********************************************************************)  

Terminating ==
    /\ phase = "commit"
      \/ phase = "abort"

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<phase,messages>>

    /\ leftForkOwner \subseteq [f \in 1..NumPHILOS |

    /\ rightForkOwner \subsete[?]

    /\ RIGHT_FORK_INIT: forall f in 1..NumPhil... etc.

name: Lamport's Bakery Algorithm
generated_spec:
---- MODULE Bakery ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT n   (* Number of processes *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables:
\*    num[p] : current ticket number held by process p (or 0 when not choosing)
\*    flag[p]: boolean indicating whether process p is currently selecting a ticket
VARIABLES num, flag

vars == <<num, flag>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
TypeOK ==
        /\ num \in [1 .. n -> Nat]
        /\ flag \in [1 .. n -> BOOLEAN]

Init ==
        /\ num = [p \in 1 .. n |-> 0]
        /\ flag = [p \in 1 .. n |-> FALSE]

Choose(p) ==
        LET maxNum == MAX(num[1..n])
            newTicket == IF maxNum # 0 THEN maxNum + 1 ELSE 1 IN
          /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p]'=TRUE]
          /\ UNCHANGED[num]
        \/ /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p]'=FALSE]
           /\ num'[p] = newTicket
           /\ UNCHANGED[[q \in 1 .. n |-> q /= p][num[q]]]

EnterCriticalSection(p) ==
        /\ flag[p] = TRUE
        /\ FORALL r \in 1 .. n :
                (\(r#p => 
                    ((num[r]=0) \/  
                     (IF num[r]<num[p] THEN TRUE

                      (* If equal numbers then lower id wins *) 
                       THEN r < p)))
               )
        /\ UNCHANGED <<flag, num>>

ExitProcess(p) ==
        /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![p]='\*']
        /\ UNCHANGED[<<flag>>]

Next ==
     \E  p \in 1 .. n : Choose(p)
   \/  \A  p \in 1 .. n : EnterCriticalSection(p)
   \/ Terminating

Terminating == /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ [] Next /\\ TypeOK

name: Producer-Consumer Queue
generated_spec:
---- MODULE BoundedFIFO ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences

CONSTANT K   (* Capacity of the queue *)

VARIABLE q      (* Sequence representing contents *), head, item, q, size, tail
          head    (* Index for next dequeue position *)
          tail    (* Index for next enqueue position *)
          size    (* Current number of elements *)

vars == <<q, head, tail, size>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
TypeOk ==
    /\ q \subseteq Nat            (* Elements are natural numbers *)
       /\ Len(q) = size           (* Size matches sequence length *)
       /\ head \in 1 .. K + 1     (* Circular indices in [1,K+1] *)
       /\ tail \in 1 .. K + 1
       /\ size \in 0 .. K         (* Number of items within bounds *)

Init ==
    /\ q = []                     (* Empty queue initially *)
    /\ head = 1                   (* Start at index 1 *)
    /\ tail = 2                    (* Tail points to first free slot (index 2) *)
    /\ size = 0

ProducerAction(item) ==
    LET newTail == IF tail # K THEN tail + 1 ELSE 1 IN
        /\ size < K                 (* Queue not full *)
        /\ item' \notin q             (* Ensure distinctness if desired optional *)
        /\ q' = Append(SeqSubseq(q, 1..size), SeqFromList([item]))
        /\ head' = head               (* Head unchanged when enqueuing *)
        /\ tail' = newTail
        /\ size' = size + 1

ConsumerAction ==
    LET oldHeadVal == SubSequence(q, head-1, head)
          nextIndex   == IF head # K THEN head + 1 ELSE 1 IN
        /\ size > 0                  (* Queue not empty *)
        /\ q' = RemoveAt(head - 1)(q)     (* Shift sequence left by removing front element *)
           \/ UNCHANGED <<head>>       (* If we keep indices fixed: alternative approach below *)

(* Alternative consumer that keeps circular buffer semantics without shifting the whole seq:
   ConsumerCircular ==
      LET consumedItem == ElemAt(q, head)

         in 
            /\ size > 0
            /\ q' = ReplaceNth(q, head, NULL?) *?* (ignored value)
            /\ head' = nextIdx
            /\ tail' = tail
            /\ size' = size - 1
*)

Next ==
    \E item \in Nat :
        ProducerAction(item)

\vee
    ConsumerAction

Spec == Init /\
        [][ Next ]_vars \
        /\ TypeOk

name: Raft Leader Election
generated_spec:
---- MODULE RaftLeaderElection ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT ServerSet
\* For simplicity we assume server identifiers are natural numbers from 1 up to MaxServerId
CONSTDEF MAXSERVERID : Nat  

ASSUME ServerSet = {s | s \in 1 .. MAXSERVERID}

VARIABLES votedFor, state, TLC, currentTerm
StateVal == {"Follower","Candidate","Leader"}

TypeOK ==
    /\ currentTerm \in Int          (* bounded by TLC's finite range assumption on INT *)
    /\ (votedFor \in ServerSet \/ votedFor = NULL)
    /\ state \in StateVal

Init ==
    /\ TypeOK
    /\ currentTerm = -1              \* initial term before any elections start
    /\ votedFor = NULL               \* no one has been voted yet
    /\ state = "Follower"

VoteRequest(cand) ==
    LET newTerm == cand.term IN

            /\ currentTerm' = newTerm
            /\ votedFor' = cand.id
            /\ UNCHANGED vars[3]      \* keep state unchanged for this simple model
        ELSEIF newTerm < currentTerm THEN
            /\ UNCHANGED vars           \* reject vote: nothing changes


                /\ UNCHANGED vars     \* cannot grant another vote in same term

                /\ votedFor' = cand.id
                /\ UNCHANGED <<currentTerm, state>>

CandidateStart(id) ==
    /\ state = "Follower"
    /\ currentTerm' = currentTerm + 1
    /\ votedFor' = id          \* self-vote
    /\ state' = "Candidate" 

LeaderCommit ==
    /\ state = "Candidate"
    /\ majorityVotesReceived()
    /\ state' = "Leader"
    /\ UNCHANGED <<votedFor,currentTerm>>  

majorityVotesReceived() ==
    (* In the simplified spec we assume an oracle function that returns TRUE when enough votes are received.
       For TLC to check properties it must be bounded. We use a nondeterministic choice over {TRUE,FALSE}. *)
    CHOOSE b : (b \in BOOLEAN)

Next ==  
    \/ CandidateStart(ANY)
    \/ VoteRequest(CANDIDATE_MSG)
    \/ LeaderCommit

(* Dummy message structures for modeling purposes *)

CANDIDATE_MSG == [id: ServerSet term: Int]
ANY == ANY

Spec == Init /\ []_(vars)[[ Next ]]

name: Read-Write Lock
generated_spec:
---- MODULE ReadWriteLock ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences

CONSTANT MAX_READERS   (* maximum number of simultaneous readers *)

VARIABLES readers, writerActive

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Readers count; integer between 0 .. MAX_READERS inclusive
TypeOk ==
    readerCountInRange == 
        (\E r : 1 <= r <= MAX_READERS) \/ TRUE  -- placeholder for range check
    writersAreBoolean == writerActive \in BOOLEAN 

vars == <<readers, writerActive>>

Init ==
    /\ readers = 0
    /\ writerActive = FALSE

Next ==
    LET nextReaders == IF writerActive THEN readers ELSE readers' IN
    CASE
      | /* Reader requests entry */
          /\ ~writerActive
          /\ (readers < MAX_READERS)
          /\ readers' = readers + 1
          /\ writerActive' = writerActive

       []/* Writer attempts acquisition when no active writer and zero readers*/
          /\ (~writerActive)
          /\ (readers = 0)
          /\ writerActive' = TRUE
          /\ UNCHANGED [reader := reader]

     []/* Writer releases lock after completion */
         /\ writerActive
         /\ writerActive' = FALSE
         /\ UNCHANGED [readers]

     []/* Reader exits the critical section */
         /\ (readers > 0)
         /\ readers' = readers - 1
         /\ UNCHANGED [writerActive] 

     [] Terminating   (* allow system to terminate *)
           /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

name: Distributed Snapshot (Chandy-Lamport)
generated_spec:
---- MODULE DistributedSnapshot ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT P, OutNeighbors
VARIABLES procState, recChanMsgs, recorder, chanMsg


(* Types *)
TypeInvariant ==
    /\ procState : P -> {"unrecorded","recorder"}
    /\ chanMsg : (OutChannels x InChannels) -> Sequence[Message]
    (* For simplicity we assume Message is just an atom no type needed *)

Init ==
    /\ \A p \in P :
          \/ procState[p] = "unrecorded"
            /\ UNCHANGED <<chanMsg>>
    /\ recorder = NULL
    /\ recChanMsgs = {}

Next == 
    LET nextProc(p) == IF procState[p]="unrecorded" THEN
                        /* process starts a new snapshot */
                          /\ recorder' = p
                          /\ procState'[p] = "recorder"
                      ELSEIF procState[p]="recorder" AND recorder'=NULL THEN
                            /* recording finished for this process: send markers to all outgoing neighbors*/
                              /\ SEND_MARKERS_TO_OUTGOING(p)

    in  /\ (\E p \in P : nextProc(p))
      /\ UNCHANGED <<procState, recorder>>   // other variables unchanged unless explicitly updated below

SEND_MARKERS_TO_OUTGOING(proc) ==
    /\ \A q \in OutNeighbors(proc):
           chanMsg'[(proc,q)] = Append(chanMsg[(proc,q)], Marker)
       /\ UNCHANGED [x |-> y IN {proc} UNION OUTCHANNELS  \union  RECCHANMS]

(* Termination *)
Terminating ==
     /\ recorder = NULL
     /\ UNCHANGED <<procState, chanMsg>>

Spec == Init /\\ [] ( Next \/ Terminating ) 

====

name: Token Ring
generated_spec:
---- MODULE TokenRing ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT N   (* number of nodes *)

VARIABLE tpos      (* current owner of the token integer from 1 .. N *), msg, tpos
                      NIL means no pending message *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Constants for convenience
TokenPositions == {i : i \in 1..N}
MsgValues     == {"msg" , @}    \* '@' denotes nil \div empty message

TypeOk ==
        /\ tpos \in TokenPositions
        /\ msg \in MsgValues

Init ==
        /\ tpos = 1          \* start at node 1
        /\ msg = @           \* initially nothing is transmitted

Next ==
        \/ (tpos < N) => 
            /\ tpos' = tpos + 1
            /\ IF msg # @ THEN UNCHANGED msg ELSE msg' = @
               * If there was an outgoing message it stays until delivered.
        \/ (tpos >= N) =>
            /\ tpos' = 1
            /\ IF msg # @ THEN UNCHANGED msg ELSE msg' = @
        \/ (\E m \in MsgValues :
                /\ msg = @         \* only when idle can send a new one
                /\ msg' = m)
        \/ Terminating   \* allow deadlock if desired: system may stop.

Terminating ==
        /\ UNCHANGED <<tpos, msg>>

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<tpos,msg>>

name: Simple Key-Value Store
generated_spec:
---- MODULE KeyValueStore ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS Keys, Values, KEY, VALUES, Value

\* Variables:
VARIABLE kvs, range, KV, kvs

vars == <<kvs>>

Init ==
    /\ kvs \subseteq [Key |-> Value]          \* type constraint for safety
    /\ DOMAIN kvs = Keys                      \* all keys present in map
    /\ FORALL k \IN Keys : kvs[k] \in VALUES     \* initial arbitrary assignment

TypeOk ==
    /\ kvs \subseteq [KEY -> VALUE]
    /\ DOMAIN kvs = KEYS
    /\ FORALL k \In KEYS : kvs[k] \in VALUES

PutNext(key, val) ==
    LET newKVS == [kv IN KV'SUBSET |
                    IF kv.key # key THEN kv ELSE <key,val> ]  \* update entry
        (* Note: we construct an updated record *)
    IN
       /\ UNCHANGED vars[1..0]      \* no other variables exist yet
       /\ kvs' = (IF key \in DOMAIN(kvs)

                       UNION {<key,val>}


GetNext(key) ==
   /\ UNCHANGED vars           \* state unchanged on read; value returned externally

\* Next disjunction:
Next ==
    \/  \E k \in Keys v \in Values :
          PutNext(k,v)

Spec ==
    Init /\
    TypeOK /\
    [][][Next]_vars

name: Paxos Single-Decree
generated_spec:
---- MODULE SingleDecreePaxos ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Sequences

CONSTANT NumAcceptors, NumProposers, ACCEPTOR_IDS, BALLLOT_NUMBERS, PROPOSER_IDS
    /* Bound for ballots so they are enumerable */
    BallotBound > 0 

VARIABLES 
    pBallots      ,  (* mapping from each proposer id -> current proposal number *), accAcks, accPromises, pValues, proposer
    pValues       ,  (* proposed values by each proposer may change until accepted *)

    accPromises   ,  (* map[acceptorId] : [ballot |-> {maxPromised} ]*)
    accAcks        ,  (* map[accid][proposalNumber]->{value,votedByMaxB} *)

(* Helper constants *)
PROPOSER_IDS \in 1..NumProposers
ACCEPTOR_IDS \in 1..NumAcceptors
BALLOT_NUMBERS == 1 .. BallotBound

(********************************************************************)
(** Type invariants **)

TypeOK ==
    /\ pBallots \subseteq PROPOSER_IDS --> BALLOT_NUMBERS
    /\ pValues \subseteq PROPOSER_IDS --> {"v" }          // placeholder value set

    /\ accPromises \subseteq ACCEPTOR_IDS --> (BALLOT_NUMBERS --> BOOLEAN)   
         \/ (\a IN ACCEPTOR_IDS: accPromises[a] = <<>>)     // no promise yet

    /\ accAcks \subseteq ACCEPTOR_IDS -->
           ((\b IN BALLLOT_NUMBERS): ({}) )                 // empty ack sets initially

(********************************************************************
** Initial state **
*)

Init ==
    /\ pBallots' = [p \in PROPOSER_IDS |->
            CHOOSE b \in BALLLOT_NUMBERS WITH TRUE]
       /* each proposer starts with some ballot number */

    /\ pValues' = [p \in PROPOSER_IDS |-> "initVal"]   (* arbitrary initial values *)

    /\ accPromises' = [ a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS |
                        IF FALSE THEN [] ELSE 
                           [b \in BALLLOT_NUMBERS |\> FALSE]]  
        /* all promises false at start */

    /\ accAcks'  = [ a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS |
                     [b \in BALLLOT_NUMBERS |>\ {} ] ]


(* Helper predicates *)

HighestPromised(acc, ballotsSet) == 
    LET maxB == MAX { b : b \in ballotsSet } 
    IN
      /\ EXISTS v,votedByMax :
          (accAcks[acc][maxB]) = <v , votedByMax>

PreparePhase(pid,pballot,val) ==
    /\ pBallots[p] <= pballot                (* proposer's current proposal no less than new one? *)
    /\ UNCHANGED <<pValues>>
    /\ (\a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS:
           CASE 
             /\\ accPromises[a][pballot] => * // already promised for this or higher?
                 UNCHANGED <<
                      accPromises,
                      accAcks >>

            \/ TRUE =>
              /\ accPromises'[a][pballot] := TRUE
              /\ accAcks'[a][pballot]   := {}
               )

AcceptPhase(pid,bval) ==
    /\ UNCHANGED <<pBallots>>   
    /\ (\a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS:  

            /\ accAcks'  [a][pBallots[pid]]
                  := <bval,{ pid }>

            UNCHANGED <<accPromises,accAcks>>

(* Next state *)

Next ==

(*
Proposer initiates a Prepare with its ballot number and value.
*)
\* Disjuncts must cover all variables; we use prime notation.

\/ (/* Propose action */)
      LET pid IN PROPOSER_IDS :
          PREPARE_ACTION(pId,pBallots[p], pValues[p])  

/\ (* Accept phase after promises *)
     LET pid IN PROPOSER_IDS : 
       ACCEPT_PHASE(pid)

Spec == Init /\ []<>(Next)_vars

name: Bounded Retransmission Protocol
generated_spec:
---- MODULE FileTransfer ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT CHUNK_SIZE, FILE_LENGTH, PACKET_ID, PACKET_IDS

    /* Maximum number of retries for each chunk */
    MAX_RETRY     >= 1,

    /* Set of all possible packet identifiers (chunk indices) */
    PACKET_IDS      : SUBSET Nat,

    /* Channel capacity is unlimited but we model it explicitly */
    CHANNEL_CAPACITY : Nat 

/* Derived constants */
VARIABLES 
    sentChunks ,          (* Sequence of packets already transmitted *), ackedPackets, channelBuffer, currentChunkIndex, retryCount
    ackedPackets ,        (* Subset of PACKET_IDS acknowledged by receiver *)
    retryCount ,         (* Mapping from PACKET_ID -> Int representing remaining attempts *)
    currentChunkIndex ,  (* Index into sequence of data being sent *)
    channelBuffer        (* Queue modeling the unreliably transmitting channel *)

(* Type constraints and invariants *)
TypeOK == /\ sentChunks \in Seq(PACKET_IDS)
           /\ ackedPackets \subseteq PACKET_IDS
           /\ UNCHANGED <<sentChunks>>
           /\ forall p \in PACKET_IDS:
                (\E r: r = retryCount[p] \/ r # retryCount[p])  
               /\ retryCount[p] \in 0..MAX_RETRY + 1
           /\ currentChunkIndex \in 0 .. Len(sentChunks)+CHANNEL_CAPACITY
           /\ channelBuffer \in Seq(Seq(Nat))

Init ==
/* Initially no chunks are in flight, nothing has been received,
   each chunk can be retried MAX_RETRY times. The sender starts with first packet index zero.
*/
\land sentChunks = []
\land ackedPackets = {}
\and retryCount = [p |-> (IF p <= FILE_LENGTH THEN MAX_RETRY ELSE -1)]
\land currentChunkIndex = 0 
\land channelBuffer = []

SendPacket(p) ==
/\ p \notin sentChunks
\/ p > LEN(channelBuffer)
/\
channelBuffer' = Append(channelBuffer , [[p]])
retryCount'[p] = MAX_RETRY

AckReceived(p) ==
/*
 * Non-deterministic message loss is modeled by allowing the receiver to either deliver or drop an ACK.
 */
UNLESS p \# IN CHANNEL_BUFFER then skip else (
    /* Receiver acknowledges and removes from buffer if present */
    UNCHANGED <<sentChunks>>
    /\ ackedPackets' = Insert(ackedPackets,p)
    /\ channelBuffer' = RemoveFirstWhere(\x: x[1]=p)(channelBuffer)

)

/*\* Retransmission logic for a single packet **/
RetransmitIfNeeded(p) ==  
/* If we have not yet exceeded retries, send again otherwise give up on this packet */   
((retryCount[p]>0) => SendPacket(p))
 \/ ((retryCount[p]<=0) =>
     (* Give up – no further action needed. *)
      UnchangedVars )

UnchangedVars == /\ sentChunks'=sentChunks
                 /\ retryCount'=retryCount
                 /\ channelBuffer'=channelBuffer

Next ==
(* The sender can choose one of several actions:
   1. Transmit next chunk (if any remain).
   2. Process acknowledgment received in channel buffer.
   3. Retry sending packets that are still pending but lost.
*)
\/ 
/\ currentChunkIndex < FILE_LENGTH          // there remains data to be transmitted
/\
SendPacket(currentChunkIndex+1)
/\ currentChunkIndex' = currentChunkIndex + 1

\\/ 
/\ Len(channelBuffer)>0                      /* There is at least an ACK waiting */
\\ AckReceived([first |-> first][currentChannel] ) 

\\/ 
FORALL p \in PACKET_IDS :
    (\E r : r=retryCount[p]) -> (
        IF retryCount[p]>0 THEN RetransmitIfNeeded(p)

       )

/* Termination: if all chunks have been acknowledged or given up, we stop transmitting */  
Terminating ==
UNLESS ackedPackets # PACKET_IDS \/ EXISTS p:\(p <=FILE_LENGTH) & retryCount[p]<=-1 then skip else UnchangedVars  

Spec == Init /\\ [][ Next ]_<< sentChunks ,ackdPackets ,retryCount ,
                               currentChunkIndex ,channelBuffer >> /\ TypeOK

====

name: Transaction Isolation (Snapshot Isolation)
generated_spec:
---- MODULE SnapshotIsolation ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, TLC, Integers

CONSTANT DBKeys, Key, SEQ, Val
VARIABLES tid, transaction, txs


(* Types *)

TypeDBKey      == [key : DBKeys]
TypeValue       == Nat        (* For simplicity values are natural numbers *)
TypeVersion     == Nat         (* Version number for each key in the database *)

\* Database is mapping from Key -> Value and version
dbRecord(key) ==
    <<value |-> dbState[key].val,
      ver   |-> dbState[key].ver>>

TxId           == Nat            (* Transaction identifiers *)
TxStatus       == {“running”, “committed”, “aborted”}
ReadSetEntry   == [txid  : TxId ,
                   key   : DBKeys ,
                   val   : TypeValue ,
                   ver   : TypeVersion ]
WriteSetEntry  == [txid  : TxId ,
                    key   : DBKeys ,
                    newVal: TypeValue ]

TransactionRec =
[ id      : TxId 
  status  : TxStatus 
  readset : Seq(ReadSetEntry)
  writeset: Seq(WriteSetEntry)
]

InitDb(dbVals) ==
    /\ \A k \in DBKeys :
        (k \mapsto <<val|->dbVals[k], 
                     ver|->[0]>>) \subseteq dbState

InitTx(txsList, nextID) ==
    txs = tlist2seq(txsList)

(* Helper to convert a set of transactions into sequence preserving order by ID *)
tlist2seq(ts) ==

         LET minT == CHOOSE tid IN DOMAIN ts:
                      MIN (\{tid' \mid tid'\in DOMAIN ts\})
          IN <<<ts[minT]]> , tlist2seq(\{i | i in DOMAIN ts & i /= minT})>


TypeOK ==
    /\ dbState \in SUBSET(DBKeys -> [value: TypeValue,
                                     ver  : TypeVersion])
    /\ txs     \in SEQ(TransactionRec)

Next ==
    \/ BeginStep
    \/ ReadStep
    \/ WriteStep
    \/ CommitAttempt
    \/ AbortStep
    \/ Terminating

BeginStep ==
    /* Start new transaction with unique id */
    let maxId == MAX({tx.id | tx <- txs} \cup {1-1})   (* if no tx yet use -1? but we need >=0 so start at 0*)
        newId == (IF maxId=-1 THEN 0 ELSE maxId+1)
      IN 
           /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState, txs>>
           /\ txs = Append(txs, 
               <<id->newId ,
                 status->"running",
                 readset->[],
                 writeset->[ ]>> )

ReadStep ==
    LET txn == CHOOSE t \IN txs :
                t.status="running"
            keyToRead == CHOICE k \in DBKeys:
                            TRUE          (* nondeterministically pick a key *)
         IN
             /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState, txs[txn.index].writeset >>
             /\ dbRecord(keyToRead) != NULL
             /\ txs' =
                  [t in DOMAIN(txns):

                          <| txn |
                             readset -> Append(
                                 txn.readset,
                                 <<txid->txn.id ,key->keyToRead,
                                   val->dbState[keyToRead].val,
                                   ver->dbState[keyToRead].ver>>)|
                         |>


WriteStep ==
    /* Transaction adds an entry to its write set */
    let txn == CHOOSE t\IN txs : t.status="running"
        targetKey   == CHOICE k \in DBKeys:TRUE
        newValue     == CHOICE v \in 0..10:TRUE      (* bounded value space for simplicity *)
       IN 
           /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState>>
           /\ txs = [
               i \in DOMAIN(txs):  

                     <| txn |
                        writeset ->
                           Append( txn.writeset ,
                               <<txid->txn.id,key->targetKey,newVal->newValue>> ) |>   


CommitAttempt ==
    LET committingTx == CHOOSE t \IN txs :
                            t.status ="running"         

         conflict? == EXISTS w1,w2 \in SEQ(committingTx.writeset) :
                      (w1.key=w2.key & w1.txid /= w2.txid)

         -- For each key in the transaction's readset, check if version changed since snapshot.
         stale?(k,v,rVer) ==
             dbState[k].ver > rVer 

         stalenessExists ==
            EXISTS rsEntry \in Seq(committingTx.readset):
                (\E kv,\vr:\exists e\in Seq(dbRecord(rsEntry.key)):
                    FALSE)
              /* placeholder actual logic below */

         validReadSet ==
            ~stale?

        IN
           \/  /\ !conflict?
                  /\ !validReadSet   (* no conflicts and reads are still consistent *)
                  /\ UNCHANGED<<dbState>>
                  /\ txs' = [i \in DOMAIN(txs) |-> IF i=commitingTx.index THEN <| commitingTx | status -> "committeed">>]

           \/  /\ TRUE      (* abort on any failure: write-write or stale read set *)
               /\ txs'= [
                   i \in DOMAIN(txns):

                         <| txn |
                            status->"aborted"|>


AbortStep ==  
    /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState>>   
    /\ txs' =
          [t \IN DOMAIN(txs):
                 IF t.status="running"
                      THEN <| t |status->"aborted">


Terminating ==
     /\ UNCHANGED dbState,txs

Spec == InitDb(DBKeys) /\
       [][][Next]_vars

name: Clock Synchronisation
generated_spec:
---- MODULE ClockSync ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT NumNodes, MaxOffset
VARIABLES clocks, offsets


(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)    
\* Types and invariants -------------------------------------------------

TypeInvariant ==
    /\ len(offsets) = NumNodes
    /\ len(clocks) = NumNodes
    /\ (\A i \in DOMAIN offsets : -MaxOffset <= offsets[i] <= MaxOffset)
    /\ (\A j \i n DOMAIN clocks : clocks[j] >= 0)

(\* All variables must be finite sets; we restrict them accordingly.\*)

vars == <<offsets, clocks>>

Init ==
    /\ TypeInvariant
    /\ offsets' = [k \in 1..NumNodes |-> 0]
    /\ clocks'  = [j \in 1..NumNodes |-> 0]

Next ==
    \/ StepDrift            (* apply random bounded drift to all nodes *)
    \/ SyncRound             (* perform a synchronization round *)
    \/ Terminating           (* allow termination if desired *)

StepDrift ==
        LET newOffsets == [n \in 1..NumNodes |

                                offsets[n] + RandomChoice(-MaxOffset .. +MaxOffset)

                               offsets[n]]
         IN  
          /\ UNCHANGED clocks
          /\ offsets' = newOffsets

SyncRound ==
      LET avgClock   == (SUM_{c \in DOMAIN clocks} clocks[c]) \div Len(DOMAIN clocks)
          delta       == { c \in DOMAIN clocks :

                              Sign(avgClock - clocks[c])

                             0 }
          updatedClks == [i \in DOMAIN clocks |-> clocks[i]+delta(i)]
     IN
          /\ clocks' = updatedClks
          /\ UNCHANGEd offsets

Terminating ==
    /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ []<>(Next)

====

name: Peterson's Algorithm
generated_spec:
---- MODULE Peterson ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT n \doteq 2   (* number of processes *)

VARIABLES flags, turn

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Flags are booleans indicating intent to enter critical section
flags \in [1 .. n -> BOOLEAN]

turn \in {1 , 2}

Init ==
    /\ flags = [i \in 1 .. n |-> FALSE]
    /\ turn = 1

Next ==
    \/ Process(1)
     \/ Process(2)

    LET j \be i # 1 + 1 IN        \* other process index *

      /\ flags[i] := TRUE          \
         /\ turn' := j             \
         /\ UNCHANGED <<flags[j]>>
       \/ * Wait until it is not my turn or opponent leaves*
           (turn#=i => ~flags[j]) 
            /\ UNCHANGED <<flags>>
       \/ * Enter CS and leave immediately after execution*
           /\ UNCHANGED <<flags>>  
           /\ UNCHANGED <<turn>>

Spec == Init /\
        [][][ Next ]_<<flags, turn>>

TypeOK ==
    /\ flags \subseteq DOMAIN[BOOLEAN]
    /\ turn \in {1 , 2}

name: Gossip Protocol
generated_spec:
---- MODULE GossipProtocol ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS NodeSet, Node
VARIABLES infection


(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Initial condition:
Init ==
    /\ infection = [n \in NodeSet |-> {}]   (* no one knows anyone yet *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Each round consists of two phases for each node:
\* Phase 1 – send phase: choose another node uniformly at random,
\*                then spread your entire set of infections to them;
\* Phase 2 – receive phase: merge received information into own state.
Next ==
    \/ (\E sender \in NodeSet :
            (\E receiver \in NodeSet : receiver # sender ) ->
              LET recvInfo == infection[sender] IN
                  /\ infection' =
                        [ n \in NodeSet |
                            IF n = receiver THEN (recvInfo  \union  infection[n])

                    UNCHANGED <<>>)
      \/ Terminating                     (* allow termination if desired *)

Terminating ==
    /\ UNCHANGED vars                 (* system may stop without changing anything *)

Spec == Init /\ []<>(Next)     (* liveness is expressed separately as a temporal property, not in Spec itself *)

TypeOK ==
    /\ NodeSet \subseteq Nat 
       /\ NodeSet /= {}
    /\ infection \in [Node -> SUBSET Node]
        /\ DOMAIN infection = NodeSet

name: Simple Allocator
generated_spec:
---- MODULE MemoryAllocator ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N, Client
\* Enumerate all possible client identifiers; for simplicity we bound it by M
M           \\ Maximum number of distinct clients  

ASSUME N >= 1
ASSUME M >= 1

VARIABLES allocated, client, free
(* Types *)

Pages   == {p | p : Nat & 1 <= p /\ p <= N}
Clients== {c | c : Nat & 1 <= c /\ c <= M}

TypeOK ==
    /\ free      \subseteq Pages
    /\ allocated \in [Client -> SUBSET Pages]
    /\ (\forall c \in Clients :
            (allocated[c] \subseteq Pages) /\
            (* No duplicate allocation across different clients *)   
            (\exists! x \in Pages : \/(\wedge i,j \in Clients:
                    i /= j => ~(x \in allocated[i]) /\ ~((i = c)/\(j=c)))))

(*
The last part ensures that each page appears in at most one client's set.
*)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
(* Initial state: no allocations and every page is free. *)

Init ==
    /\ free        = Pages
    /\ allocated' = [c \in Clients |-> {}]

Next ==
\* A request to allocate a single free page for client `req`
/\ reqReq? =>
     LET availablePage IN CHOOSE p \in free |
         TRUE
     BECAUSE availablePage # NULL
     THEN 
          /\ free           := free - <<availablePage>>
          /\ allocated'[req]:= allocated[req] #<<availablePage>>  


\/ Release of all pages held by client rel back into the pool.
/\ relRel?
   /\ releasedPages  = allocated[rel]
   /\ free      ':= free U releasedPages
   /\ allocated'[rel]:= {}

\/ Termination disjunction (to avoid deadlock)
\/ Terminating
   /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ []<>(vars) \/ Next)_vars ???

name: Publish-Subscribe Broker
generated_spec:
---- MODULE Broker ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Sequences

CONSTANT Topics, SubIds, PubIds, MessageId
VARIABLES delivered, msgSeqs, subs


(* Types *)
TypeOk ==
    \/ subs : [Topic |-> SUBSET(SubIds)]
       /\\ msgSeqs : [Topic |-> Seq(Message)]          (* ordered list of messages yet undelivered *)
       /\\ delivered :
            [SubscriberId |
                [MessageId ->
                    BOOLEAN]]                         (* true iff this sub got that Msg *)

MsgRec == <<msgid: Nat,
           pubid: PubIds,
           payload: STRING>>

Init ==
    /\ subs = [{t \in Topics} t \mapsto {}]
    /\ msgSeqs = [{t \in Topics} t \mapsto []]     \* empty sequences
    /\ delivered =
        {sub \in SubIds |
             sub \mapsto {[m.msgid \in 1..0]: FALSE}}   \* no message ids known initially

Register(sub, topic) ==
    /\ sub \in SubIds
    /\ topic \in Topics
    /\ UNCHANGED <<pubids>>
    /\ subs' = [subs EXCEPT ![topic] = @ UNION {sub}]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<msgseqs,delivered>>  

Post(pub, topic, payld) ==
    /\ pub \in PubIds
    /\ topic \in Topics
    /\ let newID = IF Len(msgSeqs(topic))=0 THEN 1 ELSE (Last(msgSeqs(topic)).msgid)+1 in 
         \/ msgSeqs' = [msgSeqs EXCEPT ![topic]=Append(@,{<<newID,pub,payld>>}) ]
            /\\ subs' = subs          (* unchanged *)
            /\\ delivered' = delivered
       /\ UNCHANGED vars

DeliverOne ==
    LET topicsWithMsgs == {t \in Topics : Len(msgSeqs(t)) > 0}
    IN  
      /\ EXISTS t \in topicsWithMsgs :
           /\ subscribers := subs[t]
           /\ msgsToSend := Head(msgSeqs[t])
           /\ FORALL s \in subscribers:
                delivered'[s][msgsToSend.msgid] = TRUE
           /\ msgSeqs' = [msgSeqs EXCEPT ![t] = Tail(@)]
        /\ Unchanged(vars)

(* Termination disjunct to avoid deadlock when no actions possible *)    
Terminate ==
     /\ UNCHANGED <<subs,msgseqs,delivered>>

Next ==
   \/ Register(sub,\*sub\*,topic)              \\ placeholder for actual parameters
   \/ Post(pub,\*pub*\ , topic, payld)
   \/ DeliverOne
   \/ Terminate

Spec == Init /\ [] ( Next )_vars

name: Dekker's Algorithm
generated_spec:
---- MODULE DekkersAlgorithm ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT P1, P2
VARIABLES turn, want


(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables:
\*   - turn : {P1,P2}          -- whose turn it currently is  
\*   - want : [{{P1}, {P2}} -> BOOLEAN]   
\*
\* The array 'want'[p] indicates whether process p wishes to enter its critical section  

TypeOk ==
    /\ turn \in {{P1},{P2}}
    /\ want = <<TRUE/False>>? (* placeholder will refine below *)

Init ==
    /\ turn \in {{P1},{P2}}
    /\ want[P1] = FALSE
    /\ want[P2] = FALSE

Next ==
    \/ ProcessOneEntry
    \/ ProcessTwoEntry
    \/ ProcessOneExit
    /\\ Terminating      \* allow dead‑lock free termination if desired

ProcessOneEntry == 
    /\ want' = [[w IN {P1,P2}] |-> IF w=P1 THEN TRUE ELSE want[w]]
    /\ UNCHANGED turn
    /\ (turn'=P1)

ProcessTwoEntry == 
    /\ want' = [[w IN {P1,P2}] |-> IF w=P2 THEN TRUE ELSE want[w]]    
    /\ UNCHANGEd turn
    /\ (turn'=P2) 

(* Exiting processes set their flag back to false *)
ProcessOneExit  == 
    /\ want'[P1]=FALSE
    /\ UNCHANGED [turn, want[~P1]]

ProcessTwoExit ==  
    /\ want'[P2]=FALSE
    /\ UNCHANGED [turn,want[~P2]]

Terminating ==   
   /\ UNCHANGED <<want,turn>>

Spec == Init /\ [] Next

name: Eventually Consistent Counter
generated_spec:
---- MODULE GCounter ---- EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets CONSTANT NodeSet \\ Set of all nodes assumed non-empty and finite, NodeSet CONSTDEF MaxVal \\ Upper bound for any individual increment per step VARIABLES counts \\ Matrix [node -> node] holding local counters, counts, m, n (* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *) \* --- Variable domains ------------------------------------------------- TypeOk == /\ counts : [NodeSet -> [NodeSet -> Nat]] /\ (\forall n1,n2 \in NodeSet : counts[n1][n2] <= MaxVal) Init == (* Initially all entries are zero *) /\ counts = <<>> \/ (\lambda _ \in NodeSet: (\lambda _ \in NodeSet: 0)) Next == LET inc(n) == (* Increment operation performed by node 'n' *) /\ UNCHANGED <> /\ counts'[n][n] = counts[n][n] + 1 merge(m,p) == (* Merge state from peer p into m's view *) /\ UNCHANGED <> /\ UNCHANGED <

> /\ (\forall i,j \in NodeSet : counts[i][j]' = IF j IN {i} THEN MAX(counts[m][j], counts[p][j]) term == (* Termination disjunction to avoid deadlock *) /\ UNCHANGED <> IN (inc(Node)) \/ (* STRING node may increment its own slot *) (merge(ANY, ANY)) /\ term (* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *) GlobalCount == SUM_{n \in NodeSet}(MAX(\{counts[][n]\})) Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<> ====
